source_pr.title: *: support flashback dropped/truncated table
source_pr.body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiDB! Please read TiDB's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/pingcap/community/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document **BEFORE** filing this PR.
-->

### What problem does this PR solve? <!--add issue link with summary if exists-->
* Support flashback dropped/truncated table.
* Parser PR: https://github.com/pingcap/parser/pull/611
eg:
```sql
test> create table t (a int key,b int);
test> insert into t values (1,1);
test> truncate table t; -- truncate the table;
test> admin show ddl jobs 3; -- look for the start_time of the truncate job. 
+--------+---------+--------------------+----------------+--------------+-----------+----------+-----------+-----------------------------------+-----------------------------------+--------+
| JOB_ID | DB_NAME | TABLE_NAME         | JOB_TYPE       | SCHEMA_STATE | SCHEMA_ID | TABLE_ID | ROW_COUNT | START_TIME                        | END_TIME                          | STATE  |
+--------+---------+--------------------+----------------+--------------+-----------+----------+-----------+-----------------------------------+-----------------------------------+--------+
| 46     | test    | t                  | truncate table | public       | 1         | 43       | 0         | 2019-11-04 11:56:49.497 +0800 CST | 2019-11-04 11:56:49.598 +0800 CST | synced |
| 44     | test    | t                  | create table   | public       | 1         | 43       | 0         | 2019-11-04 11:56:11.647 +0800 CST | 2019-11-04 11:56:11.747 +0800 CST | synced |
| 42     | mysql   | opt_rule_blacklist | create table   | public       | 3         | 41       | 0         | 2019-11-04 11:48:04.137 +0800 CST | 2019-11-04 11:48:04.19 +0800 CST  | synced |
+--------+---------+--------------------+----------------+--------------+-----------+----------+-----------+-----------------------------------+-----------------------------------+--------+
test> select * from t;
+---+---+
| a | b |
+---+---+
test> flashback table t until timestamp '2019-11-04 11:56:49.497 +0800 CST' to t1;  
test> select * from t1;
+---+---+
| a | b |
+---+---+
| 1 | 1 |
+---+---+
test> insert into t1 values (2,2)
test> select * from t1;
+---+---+
| a | b |
+---+---+
| 1 | 1 |
| 2 | 2 |
+---+---+
test> select * from t;
+---+---+
| a | b |
+---+---+
```

### What is changed and how it works?


### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

 - Unit test


Code changes

 - Has exported function/method change

Side effects

Related changes


Release note

 -  Support flashback dropped/truncated table.

symptoms: tk.MustQuery("select * from t_p_flashback order by a;").Check(testkit.Rows("1", "2", "3")) (executor/executor_test.go)
failure_signature: tk.MustQuery("select * from t_p_flashback order by a;").Check(testkit.Rows("1", "2", "3")) (executor/executor_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE: This PR adds flashback support for dropped/truncated tables and introduces new test coverage. The patch does not include a clear flaky failure signature or a targeted stabilization technique, so avoid guessing a flakiness root cause and keep it as needs-more-evidence.
fix_pattern: N/A (insufficient evidence; no targeted stabilization change identified).

evidence: evidence: tk.MustQuery("select * from t_p_flashback order by a;").Check(testkit.Rows("1", "2", "3")) (executor/executor_test.go)
